subject: math
source_title: New General Mathematics SSS 1 by M.F. Macrae et al
learning_objectives: Solve right-angled triangle problems using sine, cosine and tangent
Calculate angles of elevation and angles of depression
Find bearings and distances using trigonometric ratios

sections.heading: Solving Right-Angled Triangle Problems
sections.content: Using the ratios sin θ = opp/hyp, cos θ = adj/hyp, tan θ = opp/adj, find unknown sides or angles in right-angled triangles. Example: a ladder 6m long leans against a wall at 80° to the horizontal. Distance of foot from wall = 6 cos 80° = 6 × 0.1736 = 1.0m. Height on wall = 6 sin 80° = 6 × 0.9848 = 5.9m.

sections.heading: Angles of Elevation and Depression
sections.content: The angle of elevation is the angle measured upward from the horizontal to a higher object. The angle of depression is the angle measured downward from the horizontal to a lower object. Example: from a window 10m above ground, the angle of depression of an object on the ground is 25.4°. Distance of object from building = 10/tan 25.4° = 10/0.4748 ≈ 21.06m.

sections.heading: Bearings and Distances
sections.content: Bearings are measured clockwise from North, given as three-digit numbers (e.g., 046°). If the bearing of X from Y is 046°, then the bearing of Y from X = 180° + 46° = 226°. Use trigonometric ratios to calculate north/south and east/west components of distances. Example: town Y is 200km from X at bearing 040°, so Y is 200 × sin 40° = 128.6km east of X.
